channel.title: Craft
channel.description: Craft is a list of hand oriented craft and technology Do we really need all those power tools?

item.title: LAP Open Wire, June 20, 2026
item.description: Welcome to Lost Art Press Open Wire, where until 5 p.m. Eastern, you can ask any and all woodworking questions, and we and your fellow readers will do our best to answer. Simply post your questions in the comments section below. But there’s a caveat today: Chris is getting ready to head to the airport...
item.source:markdown: Welcome to Lost Art Press Open Wire, where until 5 p.m. Eastern, you can ask any and all woodworking questions, and we and your fellow readers will do our best to answer. Simply post your questions in the comments section below. But there’s a caveat today: Chris is getting ready to head to the airport...

item.title: For Sale: Nice Machines from Kelly Mehler’s Workshop
item.description: When woodworker and instructor Kelly Mehler died unexpectedly last year, he left behind a shop full of meticulously maintained woodworking machines that he used in his personal work after he closed his woodworking school. The Mehler family is trying to find new homes for these machines, avoiding the chaos and unpredictability of an auction. These...
item.source:markdown: When woodworker and instructor Kelly Mehler died unexpectedly last year, he left behind a shop full of meticulously maintained woodworking machines that he used in his personal work after he closed his woodworking school. The Mehler family is trying to find new homes for these machines, avoiding the chaos and unpredictability of an auction. These...

item.title: Two Steps Forward, One Step Back
item.description:
There’s good news about two recent fires, and bad news about another. Reports of lumberyard or production shop fires affect us deeply and emotionally, even when we live nowhere near...

Source

![](https://media.popularwoodworking.com/app/uploads/2026/06/17104808/fire-copy-1024x1024.jpg)There’s good news about two recent fires, and bad news about another. Reports of lumberyard or production shop fires affect us deeply and emotionally, even when we live nowhere near...
[Source](https://www.popularwoodworking.com/editors-blog/two-steps-forward-one-step-back/)

item.title: New Book: ‘17th-Century Essex County Cupboard’
item.description: Peter Follansbee’s “17th-Century Essex County Cupboard” is about as close a description as we’ll ever get to how complex pieces of 17th-century furniture began in the forest and progressed through the workshop. This particular cupboard contains a multitude of operations: riving stock, insane amounts of tricky (and simple) joinery, drawboring, nailing, faceplate and spindle turning...
item.source:markdown: Peter Follansbee’s “17th-Century Essex County Cupboard” is about as close a description as we’ll ever get to how complex pieces of 17th-century furniture began in the forest and progressed through the workshop. This particular cupboard contains a multitude of operations: riving stock, insane amounts of tricky (and simple) joinery, drawboring, nailing, faceplate and spindle turning...

item.title: Beyond the Rope
item.description:
Craft, History, and Living Apprenticeship at Colonial Williamsburg’s Joiner Shop I just returned from a visit to The Colonial Williamsburg Foundation in Williamsburg, Virginia, and it struck me how rare...

item.title: LAP Open Wire on Saturday (June 20)
item.description: We’re cooking up answers for Open Wire this Saturday (June 20) on the Lost Art Press blog – so if you have a woodworking question, you’ll have until 5 p.m. Eastern to ask it in the comments section on the Open Wire post, which will go live at 8 a.m. Chris and I will do...
item.source:markdown: We’re cooking up answers for Open Wire this Saturday (June 20) on the Lost Art Press blog – so if you have a woodworking question, you’ll have until 5 p.m. Eastern to ask it in the comments section on the Open Wire post, which will go live at 8 a.m. Chris and I will do...

item.title: Fender Washers for Mounting Pictures
item.description:
I enjoy making picture frames but always found it a pain to use tacks or staples to hold the picture in place. Plus tacks and staples are hard to remove...

Source

item.title: Video: D.R. Dimes Reborn
item.description: D.R. Dimes was one of those famous furniture making companies that was so ubiquitous that you couldn’t imagine it ever going out of business. But it did. What I didn’t know is that it has recently been reborn as a one-person shop run by Douglas P. Dimes, the son of the founder.  The story of...
item.source:markdown: D.R. Dimes was one of those famous furniture making companies that was so ubiquitous that you couldn’t imagine it ever going out of business. But it did. What I didn’t know is that it has recently been reborn as a one-person shop run by Douglas P. Dimes, the son of the founder. The story of...

item.title: New Books Update (aka Printer Delays)
item.description: Our printer is a bit more than a month behind right now, so the three new books that are gestating are overdue. They are, in order expected, Peter Follansbee’s “17th-century Essex County Cupboard,” our “Smalls” pocket book, and volume four of “The Stick Chair Journal.” We do not know when they will be in …...
item.source:markdown: Our printer is a bit more than a month behind right now, so the three new books that are gestating are overdue. They are, in order expected, Peter Follansbee’s “17th-century Essex County Cupboard,” our “Smalls” pocket book, and volume four of “The Stick Chair Journal.” We do not know when they will be in …...

item.title: If I Would Have Been A Man
item.description:

item.title: Sandpaper Index

item.title: Antique Planes, from ‘Mouldings in Practice’
item.description: We just got the final files from Matt Bickford for a new book on making side escapement moulding planes – so I’ll be diving into the editing and design of that soon. No word just yet on when it will be published, but it’s in the batter’s circle. So I thought, “Why not take a...
item.source:markdown: We just got the final files from Matt Bickford for a new book on making side escapement moulding planes – so I’ll be diving into the editing and design of that soon. No word just yet on when it will be published, but it’s in the batter’s circle. So I thought, “Why not take a...

item.title: ShopNotes Podcast Episode 274 — The Gas Station Situation in Central Iowa
item.description:
In this episode of the ShopNotes Podcast, Phil, Logan, and John cover everything from vintage woodworking tools to the surprisingly competitive gas station scene in the Midwest. Head to the...

Head to the...

item.title: Westone Thunder I Bass Restoration
item.description:
As you’ll know, once in a while, I like to do resto project: just for fun really! I’d been hankering for a Westone bass made in the legendary Matsumoku factory and I finally managed to get my hands on one. So, this series of videos is all about bring a 44 year old bass back to life!

Part 1


Part 2


Part 3
item.source:markdown:
As you’ll know, once in a while, I like to do resto project: just for fun really! I’d been hankering for a Westone bass made in the legendary Matsumoku factory and I finally managed to get my hands on one. So, this series of videos is all about bring a 44 year old bass back to life!
Part 1
Part 2
Part 3
item.author: noreply@blogger.com (Gary Nava, Luthier)

item.title: Jack-Be-Nimble
item.description:
These two new jacks do everything the older ones do but incorporate some welcome clamp characteristics. A year or two ago I talked about some new jacks not intended for...

Source

[Source](https://www.popularwoodworking.com/toolreviews/jack-be-nimble/)

item.title: Flip-Top Tool Cart
item.description:
No more lifting your heavy tools like mortisers. To store it, just tip it over. When I make mortises, my benchtop mortiser is the go-to tool because it’s fast and...

[Source](https://www.popularwoodworking.com/projects/self-storing-mortiser-cabinet/)

item.description:
Emptying my dust collector has never been something I looked forward to. For years I wrestled with the unit to empty the contents. First, I’d remove the bag and empty...

[Source](https://www.popularwoodworking.com/tricks/dust-collector-trap-door/)

item.title: New Fulfillment Center for Lost Art Press
item.description: This week we will move our order-fulfillment operation and warehouse to a nearby structure with more than 8,000 square feet of space, plus a loading dock and dedicated space for tool assembly and production of glue and our soft wax finish. So if you place an order this week, it might be delayed several days...
item.source:markdown: This week we will move our order-fulfillment operation and warehouse to a nearby structure with more than 8,000 square feet of space, plus a loading dock and dedicated space for tool assembly and production of glue and our soft wax finish. So if you place an order this week, it might be delayed several days...

item.title: Kubb
item.description:
An outdoor game that’s part bowling, part horseshoes, part chess—and totally fun for all ages.  If you enjoy picnics by playing a relaxing lawn game with the family, you’re going...

item.title: Frank Klausz Finger Joint How-to [Video]
item.description:
It’s hard to fathom, but finger joints are a relatively new joinery method for Frank Klausz, because why not just cut dovetails when you need a box?  In fact, when...

Source

[Source](https://www.popularwoodworking.com/video/video-frank-klausz-finger-joint-how-to/)

item.title: Gel Varnish
item.description:
The (almost) perfect compromise. Most woodworkers do their finishing with one of two wipe-on/wipe-off finishes: oil/varnish blend or wiping varnish. Oil/varnish blend is a thinned mixture of boiled linseed oil...

Source
item.source:markdown:
![](https://media.popularwoodworking.com/app/uploads/2020/01/02101911/0912-ff-3-1_thickness-1-1024x766.jpg)The (almost) perfect compromise. Most woodworkers do their finishing with one of two wipe-on/wipe-off finishes: oil/varnish blend or wiping varnish. Oil/varnish blend is a thinned mixture of boiled linseed oil...
[Source](https://www.popularwoodworking.com/finishing/gel-varnish/)

item.title: Post and Rung Chairs
item.description:
Slowly removing the barriers to start building. Post and rung chairs can seem intimidating to many: the angles, the steam bending, and the seat weaving. However, this iconic Appalachian style...

Post and rung chairs can seem intimidating to many: the angles, the steam bending, and the seat weaving. However, this iconic Appalachian style...
[Source](https://www.popularwoodworking.com/editors-blog/post-and-rung-chairs/)

item.title: How to Install Butt Hinges
item.description: When I can’t afford the fancy forged hinges (or would rather spend that money elsewhere), I most often reach for 2-1/2″ butt hinges from Horton Brasses (item PB-409, about $42 per pair, including screws and shipping). And while butt hinges are available at any hardware store and at a range of price points (some incredibly...
item.source:markdown: When I can’t afford the fancy forged hinges (or would rather spend that money elsewhere), I most often reach for 2-1/2″ butt hinges from Horton Brasses (item PB-409, about $42 per pair, including screws and shipping). And while butt hinges are available at any hardware store and at a range of price points (some incredibly...

item.title: Take Your Home to Work
item.description:
A lunchtime woodworking break yields more than a nice saw handle. In 2013, I took Matt Cianci’s Build a Backsaw weekend class through my local woodworking guild. There is never...

Source

[Source](https://www.popularwoodworking.com/end-grain/take-work-home/)
